title: Permit Approved â€“ Ductor & Weaver Brothers Advance Landmark RNG & Organic Fertilizer Project in Ohio
content: Ductor Americas, Inc. and Weaver Brothers Eggs announced a major milestone for their RNG and organic fertilizer project in Darke County, Ohio, having received the "Permit to Install and Operate" from the Ohio Department of Agriculture. Located at Weaver Brothersâ€™ egg production facility, the project will utilize Ductorâ€™s proprietary nutrient recovery process to transform poultry manure into three high-value products: Renewable Natural Gas (RNG) with a low carbon intensity score (~-100 CI), certified organic liquid ammonia fertilizer, and solid fertilizer. With the permit secured, the partners will proceed to final engineering and financing, with construction expected to begin in the first half of 2026 and RNG production targeted for Q4 2027. This facility serves as the first of up to 15 planned plants across the U.S., representing a scalable model for sustainable infrastructure that targets over $1.5 billion in total investment by 2032.
author: Analysts Account
